comment.body: Here it is -${0} A few questions regarding the weekly outlook. Looking at the positioning within the 1-SD straddle (6350–6850), I’m seeing dealers in a net short -823 contract posture. Specifically, they're roughly 16k short above spot and 18k long below, effectively a short risk-reversal profile. I noticed about 63% of the week's downside contracts are front-loaded on Monday. With that Monday positioning showing a steeply negative speed profile, it seems an IV spike tomorrow should actually bolster gamma at those lower strikes, providing a 'brake' on the move.I'm struggling to reconcile the Zomma/Vomma transition. You mentioned Zomma remains heavily negative into the end of the week, yet I see dealer longs below on the Thursday/Friday tapes. Furthermore, if downside Vomma flips from heavily positive (Mon/Tue) to peaking negative on Friday, how does the Zomma stay negative through the back half of the week? Would love your help squaring the positioning data with the Greek profile shift you're forecasting
